prevalence.source: ERA-EDTA/ECDC; based on ERA-EDTA Registry 2022 data extrapolated
prevalence.trend: Increasing (1-2% annually due to aging and diabetes rise)

incidence.value: ~120 per million population (for end-stage renal disease; overall CKD incidence ~2% in high-risk groups)

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 (IHME); EU-specific adjustments

vulnerablePopulations: Ethnic minorities (e.g., South Asian, African descent)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: KDIGO 2012 guidelines: Persistent eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73m² for >3 months or evidence of kidney damage (e.g., albuminuria >30 mg/24h, structural abnormalities).

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Varies; ~25-40% in high-risk groups (e.g., diabetics) across EU countries
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~45% achieve target blood pressure control; ~30% on renin-angiotensin system blockers

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Uniform adoption of KDIGO 2012/2024 updates across EU; eGFR calculated using CKD-EPI equation
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: KDIGO-recommended: Blood pressure control (<130/80 mmHg), use of ACEi/ARBs/SGLT2 inhibitors, lifestyle interventions; progression to dialysis/transplant for stage 5
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Aging population driving prevalence; east-west disparities in access to nephrology care; rising comorbidities like obesity/diabetes
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Comprehensive renal registries (e.g., ERA-EDTA); high transplant rates (~40 per million); integrated care models in countries like Spain and Netherlands
